title: Three-Axis RCS Thruster Allocation and Torque Mapping
content: A reaction control system (RCS) for a 3-axis spacecraft uses paired cold-gas nozzles aligned with each body axis. Torque commands (Tx, Ty, Tz) from a controller are mapped to individual thruster pulse widths via a thruster allocation matrix: each axis pair fires the +axis thruster for positive torque and the -axis thruster for negative torque. Pulse width scales with |command| * dt to respect minimum impulse bit constraints. The allocation must respect factor-of-safety margins on propellant mass and solenoid duty-cycle limits (<5% average) to prevent valve overheating. Residual cross-coupling torques are rejected by the inner rate loop of a cascaded PID controller.
